name: GlyphStudio v1 ã A Governed Pixel Asset Studio
body: GlyphStudio v1 is a governed pixel asset studio for creating stills, animation, variants, reusable parts, and structured output.
This release focuses on five connected product arcs:
Palette variants, reusable parts, document variants, and batch bundle export.
Unified library, pinning and recency, search and keyboard retrieval, and interchange.
Project templates, asset packs, apply-to-project flows, and an upgraded start surface.
Starter recipes, contextual workflow hints, shortcut discovery, and canonical sample content.

name: v1.0.0
body: First stable release.
Compile repos into structured `llms.txt` Markdown documents
Auto-sync to Google Drive with idempotent file IDs
Watch mode with configurable debounce
MCP server for AI assistant integration (sync_to_drive, compile_context, sync_status, list_repos)
Smart filtering via `.gitignore`, `.llmsignore`, and built-in rules

name: Soundweave v1.0.1
body: Adaptive soundtrack studio for composing, arranging, scoring, and exporting interactive game music. Soundweave is a composition-first, adaptation-aware workstation ã game music that feels intentional, not generated.
Studio** ã 13-screen authoring UI for clips, stems, scenes, bindings, transitions, automation, and score maps
16 packages** ã Schema, audio engine, clip engine, music theory, score map, automation, library, runtime pack, and more
Adaptive logic** ã Trigger bindings, deterministic scene resolution, crossfade/stinger/cooldown transitions
World scoring** ã Motif families, score profiles, cue families, derivation chains
Sample workflow** ã Import, trim, slice, kit builder, sample instruments

name: v1.0.4
body: Starlight handbook (7 pages, 7 locales), retranslated READMEs, landing page CTA ã handbook.

name: v1.0.3
body: httpx connection pooling** ã reuse connections instead of creating one per request
Semaphore context manager** ã `async with sem:` pattern, prevents slot leaks on exceptions
Race condition fix** ã `_active` counter protected by asyncio.Lock
Cache path traversal** ã uses `relative_to()` instead of string comparison
Structured logging** ã replaces `print(stderr)` with Python logging module
Specific exceptions** ã no more bare `except Exception` in ollama.py

name: v1.0.2
body: Renamed PyPI distribution from `py-polyglot` to `polyglot-gpu` (import name `pypolyglot` unchanged)
CLI entry point renamed to `polyglot-gpu`
Added trusted publisher workflow for PyPI releases
```bash
pip install polyglot-gpu

name: v1.0.0
body: Oregon Trail-style survival game with AI Game Master and optional XRPL ledger backpack.
Procedurally generated wilderness with seeded world generation
6 camp actions: travel, rest, hunt, repair, ration, abandon cargo
AI Game Master with 3 narrative profiles (Chronicler, Fireside, Lantern-Bearer)
Rich terminal UI via Textual
Save/load with automatic checkpoint system

name: v1.7.1
body: Added branded README with logo, usage examples, and exports table to all 10 npm packages
Added LICENSE to all sub-packages for npm compliance
Complete translations: Japanese, Chinese, Spanish, French, Hindi, Italian, Portuguese
All 10 packages now live on npm at v1.7.1:
| Package | npm |

name: v1.7.0 ã Supply Chain Receipts
body: Three new receipt pipelines for security teams and executives:
registry-sync** ã Prove what's published vs what's in the repo
Drift classification: in sync, ahead, behind, unpublished
Each package becomes a receipt output with version comparison
security-audit** ã Prove what you scanned, with what tool, and what was found
Severity breakdown (critical/high/moderate/low/info) in intent and metadata

name: v1.6.0 ã Bundle Authority
body: Bundles can now be signed and their signatures verified, establishing **who created the parcel**. The zip contents are never mutated ã signatures live as detached sidecars (`.sig`/`.cert`) next to the bundle.
`rf bundle sign <file>`** ã Sign a bundle with cosign (detached sidecar)
`--keyless` for OIDC-based signing in CI
`--key <path>` for local key-based signing
`rf bundle verify --require-bundle-signature`** ã Require a valid bundle-level signature
`rf verify <file.zip> --require-bundle-signature`** ã Auto-detect passthrough

name: v1.5.0 ã Receipt Bundles
body: v1.5.0 introduces **receipt bundles** ã self-verifying zip archives that package a receipt with its entire trust chain into a single portable file.
bundle.zip/
ããã manifest.json     # Bundle metadata
ããã hashes.json       # SHA-256 of every file
ããã VERIFY.md         # Human-readable verification instructions

name: v1.4.0 ã Strict Refs, Policy Identity, Policy Signing
body: Default stays best-effort ã missing references are reported but don't fail verification. With `--refs-strict`, missing or unreadable references fail. Hash mismatches always fail regardless of mode.
```bash
rf verify receipt.json --follow --refs-strict --receipts-dir receipts/

Receipts now embed `policy_identity.hash` ã the SHA-256 of the lint policy rules applied at creation time. During verification, if a policy is supplied, the hash is compared. Mismatch = fail. This prevents policy swapping.
`PolicyIdentity` type: `hash`, optional `version`, `path`, `signed`, `signatureRef`

name: v1.3.0 ã Multi-Tenant SaaS Architecture
body: App template upgraded** to `/app/[workspace]/ãÎ` multi-tenant routing (3 seeded workspaces)
RBAC policy layer** ã `canViewBilling`, `canManageTeam`, `canManageWorkspace`
Feature flags** ã `billing`, `teams`, `auditLog`, `apiKeys` with `isEnabled()` gating
Centralized data layer** ã workspace-keyed projects, metrics, and activity
Path-preserving workspace switcher** with plan badges (starter / pro / business)
AccessDenied component** + billing page RBAC gate

name: v1.0.1 ã Handbook + Publish Workflow
body: HANDBOOK.md** ã complete operational reference (13 chapters + 3 appendices) covering architecture, commands, performance model, Docker execution, resource governance, security, tuning, troubleshooting, and design decisions
Publish workflow** ã 3-channel automated release (PyPI via OIDC, GHCR gateway image, GitHub Release artifacts)
```bash
pip install --upgrade claude-toolstack-cli

name: v1.0.0 ã Bounded Code Intelligence
body: A ready-to-deploy stack that keeps Claude Code productive on large, multi-language repositories without thrashing a 64-GB Linux workstation.
FastAPI gateway** with 6 bounded endpoints (search, slice, ctags, symbol, job runner, metrics)
systemd cgroup v2 slices** ã 5 slices enforce MemoryHigh/Max per service group
Exec-only Docker model** ã socket proxy blocks 14 higher-risk Docker endpoints
`cts` CLI** ã zero-dependency Python client with text/JSON/Claude output modes
Evidence bundles v2** ã 4 bundle modes (default, error, symbol, change) with path ranking + git recency
